claimText: SDN-1 technology involves site-directed mutagenesis without the use of a DNA sequence template.
evidenceExcerpt: SDN-1 involves site-directed mutagenesis without a DNA sequence template.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing between different types of Site Directed Nuclease technologies.
distractorHooks: Involves insertion of large foreign DNA sequences

claimText: SDN-2 technology utilizes a DNA sequence template to perform site-directed mutagenesis.
evidenceExcerpt: SDN-2 uses a DNA sequence template for mutagenesis.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching SDN types to their specific mechanisms.

claimText: SDN-3 technology involves the insertion of a gene or a large DNA sequence using a template.
evidenceExcerpt: SDN-3 involves the insertion of a gene or large DNA sequence using a template.
examUse: Statement-based question on genetic engineering techniques and their definitions.
distractorHooks: Approved in India for general crops

claimText: India's biosafety regulations have approved genome editing of SDN-1 and SDN-2 types for general crops.
evidenceExcerpt: India's biosafety regulations have approved genome editing of SDN-1 and SDN-2 types for general crops.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's regulatory framework for genetically modified and genome-edited crops.
distractorHooks: SDN-3 is approved for general crops
SDN-1 and SDN-2 are banned in India
